question: What is a physician's assistant?
gold_answer: physician assistants are medical providers who are licensed to diagnose and treat illness and disease and to prescribe medication for patients
predicted_answer:
A physician assistant is a certified and state-licensed healthcare professional who works as part of a physician's team.

[Primary Source: FixedContext, Link: https://www.collegebasics.com/blog/top-3-university-programs-physician-assistants/_p0, Chunk: https://www.collegebasics.com/blog/top-3-university-programs-physician-assistants/_p0]
fixed_context:
A physician assistant is a certified and state-licensed healthcare professional who works as part of a physician’s team.
A licensed PA can independently practice medicine and provide treatment for patients nationally.
�The Physician Assistant.
The physician assistant is a representative of the physician, treating the patient in the style and manner developed and directed by the supervising physician.
Regulatory Requirements: A physician assistant may provide medical services, which are delegated in writing by a supervising physician who is responsible for patients, cared for by the physician assistant.

question: What is the physician's assistant average salary vs a registered nurse?

question: What's the difference between flutter and fibrillation?
gold_answer: In atrial fibrillation, the atria beat irregularly. In atrial flutter, the atria beat regularly, but faster than usual and more often than the ventricles, so you may have four atrial beats to every one ventricular beat.
predicted_answer:
In atrial fibrillation, the atria beat irregularly. In atrial flutter, the atria beat regularly, but faster than usual and more often than the ventricles, so you may have four atrial beats to every one ventricular beat.

memory_entry.question: What's the difference between flutter and fibrillation?
memory_entry.exact_answer_span:
In atrial fibrillation, the atria beat irregularly. In atrial flutter, the atria beat regularly, but faster than usual and more often than the ventricles, so you may have four atrial beats to every one ventricular beat.

[Primary Source: FixedContext, Link: http://web.archive.org/web/20200523205052id_/https://www.bhf.org.uk/informationsupport/heart-matters-magazine/medical/ask-the-experts/atrial-flutter_p9, Chunk: http://web.archive.org/web/20200523205052id_/https://www.bhf.org.uk/informationsupport/heart-matters-magazine/medical/ask-the-experts/atrial-flutter_p9]

question: How happens to waters molecules when it freezes?

question: Does the effect of LASIK wear off with age?
gold_answer: In most cases the improved vision LASIK surgery provides is permanent. But in a limited number of cases — usually due to changes that can occur in the lens inside the eye, with or without LASIK surgery — some nearsightedness, farsightedness and/or astigmatism can return over time, causing blurry vision.

question: How is tofu made?
gold_answer: Tofu is made from soybeans that are ground in water, heated, and coagulated with minerals like calcium or magnesium salt. The curds are then pressed into a block, which is then sold as tofu.

question: Can tofu make you sick?
gold_answer: A refrigerated tofu is usually stored with water and when it is left in the refrigerator for too long, it will be at risk of microbial degradation. Because of this, a person who has consumed expired tofu may exhibit digestive issues as well as other symptoms that are similar to that of food poisoning.

question: Describe core, aggregator and edge switches.
gold_answer: In small networks of a few hundred users, edge switches can be connected redundantly directly to core switch/router devices. However, for larger networks, , an additional layer of switching, called the distribution layer, aggregates the edge switches.
predicted_answer:
* Core switch/router devices are connected to edge switches in small networks.

[Primary Source: FixedContext, Link: http://web.archive.org/web/20200927051817id_/https://biztechmagazine.com/article/2013/10/understanding-different-layers-routing-and-switching_p4, Chunk: http://web.archive.org/web/20200927051817id_/https://biztechmagazine.com/article/2013/10/understanding-different-layers-routing-and-switching_p4]
fixed_context:
In small networks of a few hundred users, edge switches can be connected redundantly directly to core switch/router devices. However, for larger networks, , an additional layer of switching, called the distribution
The Distribution and Aggregation Layer In small networks of a few hundred users, edge switches can be connected redundantly directly to core switch/router devices.
The aggregation layer connects to the uplinks of the edge switches and is uplinked to the distribution layer toward the network core.

question: Are potential energy and kinetic energy the same?
gold_answer: Potential Energy is the stored energy in an object or system because of its position or configuration. Kinetic energy of an object is relative to other moving and stationary objects in its immediate environment.
predicted_answer:
No, they are not the same. According to the supporting context, potential energy is completely independent of its environment, whereas kinetic energy is relative to other moving and stationary objects in its environment.

[Source: FixedContext, Link: http://web.archive.org/web/20200517211204id_/https://www.diffen.com/difference/Kinetic_Energy_vs_Potential_Energy_p0, Chunk: http://web.archive.org/web/20200517211204id_/https://www.diffen.com/difference/Kinetic_Energy_vs_Potential_Energy_p0]
fixed_context:
Energy is the stored energy in an object or system because of its position or configuration. Relation to environment Kinetic energy of an object is relative to other moving and stationary objects
While kinetic energy of an object is relative to the state of other objects in its environment, potential energy is completely independent of its environment.
Kinetic energy is energy possessed by a body by virtue of its movement .
All energy types can be sorted into two main categories: potential and kinetic.
Kinetic energy is the energy of motion.

question: What is peculiar about Uranus's seasons?
gold_answer: For two 21-year seasons on Uranus – the winter-summer seasons – the poles of Uranus are pointed (more or less) either toward the sun, or away from it. During Uranus' winter-summer season, the winter side of the planet never sees the sun. It doesn't see the sun for 21 long years.

question: Does weathering change the composition of rocks?
gold_answer: Chemical weathering may change the size of pieces of rock materials, but definitely changes the composition. So one type of mineral changes into a different mineral.
